core.label: Swift Fundamentals
core.description: Syntax, types, optionals, and basic program structure.

topics.label: Xcode & Playgrounds
topics.description: Set up Xcode and experiment with Swift Playgrounds. Playgrounds are great for learning interactively.

topics.label: Variables & Constants
topics.description: var for mutable, let for immutable. Swift encourages using let whenever possible.

topics.label: Basic Types
topics.description: Int, Double, Float, Bool, String, Character. Swift is strongly typed with type inference.

topics.label: String Interpolation & Operations
topics.description: Embed values with \(expression) and use rich string APIs. Strings are value types in Swift.

topics.label: Collections (Array, Dictionary, Set)
topics.description: Typed collections with generics. Arrays, dictionaries, and sets are the core data structures.

topics.label: Control Flow
topics.description: if/else, guard, switch (pattern matching), for-in, while, repeat-while. guard enables early returns.

topics.label: Optionals
topics.description: Represent the absence of a value with Optional (Type?). if let, guard let, and ?? for safe unwrapping.

topics.label: Functions
topics.description: Functions with argument labels, default parameters, variadic parameters, and inout. Argument labels improve readability.

topics.label: Tuples
topics.description: Group multiple values into a single compound value. Useful for function return types.

topics.label: Closures
topics.description: Self-contained blocks of code with trailing closure syntax. Closures are used everywhere in Swift APIs.

topics.label: Structs vs Classes
topics.description: Structs are value types (copied), classes are reference types (shared). Prefer structs in Swift.

topics.label: Properties
topics.description: Stored, computed, lazy, and property observers (willSet/didSet). Properties encapsulate state.

topics.label: Initializers
topics.description: Designated and convenience initializers, failable initializers (init?). Swift has strict initialization rules.

topics.label: Enumerations
topics.description: Enums with associated values, raw values, and methods. Swift enums are algebraic data types.

topics.label: Protocols
topics.description: Define contracts with protocol. Protocols with extensions enable protocol-oriented programming.

topics.label: Protocol Extensions
topics.description: Add default implementations to protocols. Protocol extensions are Swift's primary code reuse mechanism.

topics.label: Inheritance
topics.description: Class inheritance with override and final. Only classes support inheritance — structs use protocols.

topics.label: Extensions
topics.description: Add methods, computed properties, and protocol conformance to existing types. Extensions are used everywhere.

topics.label: Access Control
topics.description: open, public, internal, fileprivate, private. Control visibility across modules and files.

core.label: Generics & Pattern Matching
core.description: Generics, associated types, and advanced pattern matching.

topics.label: Generics
topics.description: Write type-safe generic functions and types with <T>. Generics are central to Swift's standard library.

topics.label: Generic Constraints
topics.description: Constrain generics with protocol conformance and where clauses.

topics.label: Associated Types
topics.description: Protocol associated types for generic protocols. Used in Collection, Sequence, and Iterator.

topics.label: Opaque Types (some)
topics.description: Return types hidden behind some Protocol. Used extensively in SwiftUI.

topics.label: Pattern Matching
topics.description: Advanced switch patterns — value binding, tuple matching, where clauses, and enum patterns.

topics.label: Result Type
topics.description: Represent success or failure with Result<Success, Failure>. Functional error handling pattern.

topics.label: Error Handling
topics.description: throw, try, catch, and do blocks. Swift errors are typed and must be handled explicitly.

topics.label: Custom Operators
topics.description: Define prefix, infix, and postfix operators. Used sparingly for domain-specific expressiveness.

core.label: Concurrency & Memory
core.description: async/await, actors, ARC, and memory management.

topics.label: async/await
topics.description: Write asynchronous code with async functions and await. Swift's structured concurrency model.

topics.label: Tasks & Task Groups
topics.description: Create concurrent work with Task and TaskGroup. Structured concurrency manages task lifecycles.

topics.label: Actors
topics.description: Protect mutable state with actor types. Actors prevent data races by isolating state.

topics.label: Sendable & @MainActor
topics.description: Mark types as safe to share across concurrency domains. @MainActor ensures UI updates on the main thread.

topics.label: ARC (Automatic Reference Counting)
topics.description: Understand how Swift manages memory for classes. ARC automatically frees unused objects.

topics.label: Strong, Weak & Unowned
topics.description: Break retain cycles with weak and unowned references. Critical for avoiding memory leaks.

topics.label: Closures & Capture Lists
topics.description: Control how closures capture references with [weak self] and [unowned self].

topics.label: AsyncSequence
topics.description: Iterate over asynchronous data with for await. Used for streams, notifications, and real-time data.

topics.label: XCTest
topics.description: Write unit tests with XCTestCase, assertions, and setup/teardown. Apple's built-in testing framework.

topics.label: Swift Testing
topics.description: Modern testing with @Test macro and #expect (Swift 5.10+). The new Swift-native testing framework.

topics.label: Swift Package Manager
topics.description: Manage dependencies and create packages with Package.swift. SPM is Swift's built-in package manager.

topics.label: Debugging & Instruments
topics.description: Use LLDB, breakpoints, and Instruments for profiling. Instruments finds memory leaks and performance issues.

topics.label: SwiftLint
topics.description: Enforce Swift coding conventions with SwiftLint. Consistent style across the team.

topics.label: Codable
topics.description: Encode and decode JSON, Plist, and custom formats with Codable. The standard serialization protocol.

topics.label: Property Wrappers
topics.description: Add logic to property access with @propertyWrapper. Used in SwiftUI (@State, @Binding, @Published).

topics.label: Macros (Swift 5.9+)
topics.description: Generate code at compile time with Swift macros. Macros reduce boilerplate and enable metaprogramming.
